jobs.title: Deputy Director of Recreation & Community Services
jobs.description:
**Description**
---------------


**POSITION:**
The Deputy Director of Recreation \& Community Services is a key leadership role responsible for overseeing the Department’s daily operations and supporting the Director of Recreation \& Community Services in strategic planning and program delivery. This position provides high\-level managerial and administrative support in the development, implementation, and evaluation of City programs, including recreation services, park programming and development, Capital Improvement Projects (CIP), community and social services, events, and facility rentals.  

The Deputy Director manages the day\-to\-day operations and plans, directs, and evaluates departmental programs, services, facilities, and operations; supervises professional and administrative staff; and ensures effective coordination across City departments and external partners. The position also serves as acting Director when designated and acts as the liaison to assigned commissions and committees.  

This role requires flexibility, including evening and weekend work, and occasional travel.
**Examples of Duties**
----------------------


**ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:**

* Under the general direction of the Director of Recreation \& Community Services, oversee the delivery of recreation programs, community services, events, and facility operations.
* Oversee front desk operations, including registration, deposits, balancing, and customer service.
* Administer the Department event calendar and manage the Community Calendar.
* Assist in managing facility maintenance and department vehicles.
* Assist in the development and implementation of departmental goals, policies, procedures, and performance standards.
* Monitor and evaluate program effectiveness, service quality, and customer experience; implement improvements and risk management practices.
* Coordinate major departmental initiatives, projects, and capital improvements; stay current with industry trends and best practices.
* Supervise, mentor, and evaluate staff; recommend hiring, discipline, and performance standards; and oversee training and professional development programs.
* Participate in and oversee the preparation and administration of departmental budgets, including revenue projections, cost recovery analysis, grants, and expenditure control.
* Provide oversight of park and facility operations and ensure alignment with City standards and community needs.
* Review and approve final departmental marketing and communications.
* Serve as a liaison to assigned advisory boards, community groups, and other stakeholders; provide technical guidance and support.
* Research, prepare, and present reports, recommendations, and presentations to the City Council, commissions, and committees.
* Analyze complex operational and community issues; develop and implement effective solutions.
* Conduct and lead the team in an annual Department program and event evaluation.
* Exercises sound judgment, prioritizes tasks, and maintains political awareness.
* Analyzes complex issues related to parks and recreation.
* Report, research, and participate in advocacy in parks and recreation legislation.
* Foster partnerships and coordinate activities with other City departments, agencies, and community organizations.
* Assists the Director in community representation at external activities, functions, and events. Attend internal City events in a department lead capacity.
* Act on behalf of the Director when designated.
* This role requires flexibility, including evening and weekend work, and occasional travel.
* Engages with the public and collaborates with Citizen advisory boards, Community Foundation, vendors and partners, City and community officials.
* Performs additional duties as assigned.

**Typical Qualifications**
--------------------------


**EXPERIENCE AND TRAINING:**
Graduation from an accredited college or university with major course work in recreation, public administration or related field with eight (8\) years of increasingly responsible full\-time experience, including four (4\) years at full\-time Manager level experience in the development and management of municipal recreation and community and social service programs, event management, and parks and recreation facilities. A Master's degree in Public Administration or Recreation Administration is highly desirable. **DESIRABLE KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES****Knowledge of:*** Principles, practices, and methods used in parks and recreation management.
* Organizational and management practices as applied to the analysis and evaluation of programs, policies, and operational needs.
* Pertinent federal, state and local laws, rules, codes, and ordinances.
* Methods and techniques of effective project management, and technical report preparation and presentation.
* Principles and practices of contract negotiation, preparation, and administration.
* Principles and practices of program organization, evaluation, development, and administration.
* Recent developments, contemporary trends and practices of parks, recreation, arts, and events.
* Operational characteristics, services and activities of recreational and community service programs.
* Principles and practices of budget preparation and administration.
* Principles and practices of personnel management, supervision, training, and performance evaluation.
* Principles and practices of leadership, motivation, team building, and conflict resolution.
* Modern office practices and technology, including personal computer hardware and software; computer\-based project management and scheduling.
* Safe work practices.
* English usage, spelling, grammar, and punctuation.

**Ability to****:*** Plan, direct, manage, and oversee the administration and operations of the Recreation \& Community Services Department, venue rentals, arts, events, programs, and activities.
* Develop, implement, and interpret goals, objectives, policies, procedures, and work stan

jobs.title: After-School Program Director (MUST HAVE PRIOR DYCD COMPASS SUPERVISORY EXPERIENCE)
jobs.description:
Organization Overview

Veronica Arts Media, Inc. is a 501c3 nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering youth through high\-quality enrichment programs that support academic success, creativity, wellness, and personal growth. Our mission is to provide students with access to engaging programs such as physical fitness, academic support, social\-emotional learning, STEAM and literacy enrichment, media arts, leadership development, and early exposure to college and career pathways. To learn more about Veronica Arts Media, visit www.veronicaartsmedia.org.

Why Join Veronica Arts Media, Inc.?

This is your chance to contribute directly to an organization committed to empowering youth through high\-quality enrichment, creativity, wellness, academic support, and life\-changing opportunities.

Position Overview

The Program Director oversees the daily operations of a COMPASS after\-school program and provides leadership and supervision to staff to ensure program goals and student outcomes are achieved. As the on\-site leader, the Program Director is responsible for the high\-quality implementation of a DYCD\-funded program (COMPASS/SONYC), with a focus on participant safety, regulatory compliance (DYCD, SACC, and organizational standards), and fiscal accountability. The Program Director serves as the primary liaison with the school principal and represents the program within the broader school community, including NYCPS staff, families, and community partners. The role leads the delivery of a dynamic program that includes academic enrichment, project\-based learning, arts, sports, leadership development, and initiatives supporting students’ academic and social\-emotional growth, including NYC Reads. This role also includes leading our Summer Rising programming each year, requiring flexibility with scheduling and location adjustments during the summer months. This is a full\-time position based in the Bronx. The school\-year schedule is Monday–Friday, 10:00 AM–6:00 PM, with additional evening and weekend availability required based on program needs.The Program Director reports to the Executive Director and Site Supervisor.

Key Responsibilities

* Ensure program activities align with DYCD COMPASS and SONYC requirements and organizational goals.
* Oversee daily site operations to maintain a safe, structured, and compliant program environment.
* Manage attendance tracking, data entry, and required documentation in DYCD Connect.
* Supervise and support site staff, including the Content Specialist, Group Leaders, and Activity Specialists.
* Conduct staff observations and evaluations and provide ongoing coaching and professional development.
* Ensure compliance with SACC regulations, including supervision ratios, safety protocols, and staff credential requirements.
* Serve as the primary point of contact with NYCPS school personnel, families, and community partners.
* Lead recruitment and retention efforts to meet DYCD enrollment and attendance targets.
* Support high‑quality programming across literacy, STEM, arts, civic engagement, and NYC Reads initiatives.
* Coordinate inclusion supports and manage Individualized Accommodation Plans (IAPs) for participants with diverse needs.
* Lead the implementation of safety drills, behavior protocols, and crisis management plans.

Requirements

* DOE Fingerprinted
* SACC Director Requirement
* Statewide Central Registry clearance
* Department of Health clearance
* Bachelor’s degree in a child\-related field (e.g., Education, Child Development, Psychology, Social Work) and at least two years of direct experience working with school\-age children; or A minimum of three (3\) years of supervisory or program management experience in youth development or educational settings.
* Two years of college with 18 credits in Child Development, Elementary Education, Physical Education, Recreation or a related field

Qualifications

* Knowledge of DYCD COMPASS and SONYC program requirements and experience working in partnership with NYCPS schools.
* Demonstrated ability to supervise staff, manage program operations, oversee budgets, and maintain regulatory compliance.
* Experience collaborating with school staff, families, and community partners.
* Strong communication, organizational, and problem\-solving skills, with a commitment to youth development, student safety, educational equity, and culturally responsive practices.

Approach to Work

* Strong commitment to educational equity
* Passion for working with students in under\-resourced schools
* Exceptional level of patience and focus to overcome challenges and achieve results

Pay and Benefits

* Commensurate with experience
* Sick and Safe Leave
* Health, dental, and vision insurance
* Select DOE holidays, depending on OST program operating schedules

Pay: From $65,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

jobs.company: United Fund for the Arts and Humanities
jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**Help Shape the Future of Arts and Humanities in Our Region**

The United Fund for the Arts and Humanities (UFAH) brings people together to strengthen the arts and humanities through collaborative fundraising.

Each year, UFAH works with donors, businesses, foundations, volunteers, and partner organizations to support programs that inspire creativity, preserve history, expand arts education, and enrich community life throughout the greater La Crosse area.

As Executive Director, you will help lead this work by strengthening community relationships, coordinating fundraising efforts, supporting good governance, and ensuring the organization operates with professionalism, accountability, and excellence.

**Position Summary**

The Executive Director serves as UFAH's primary staff leader and works closely with the Board of Directors to advance the organization's mission and strategic priorities.

This is a hands\-on leadership position within a one\-staff organization. The Executive Director provides organizational leadership while coordinating fundraising activities, supporting Board governance, managing day\-to\-day operations, maintaining administrative systems, and building productive relationships throughout the community.

Supported by an engaged Board of Directors, active committees, and dedicated volunteers, the Executive Director plays a central role in ensuring UFAH remains organized, responsive, and sustainable.

**Primary Responsibilities**

The Executive Director is responsible for:

* Coordinating UFAH's Annual Fund and donor stewardship activities.
* Managing day\-to\-day organizational operations and office administration.
* Supporting the Board of Directors and its committees through meeting coordination, governance support, and implementation of Board\-approved policies.
* Maintaining accurate donor, financial, and organizational records.
* Coordinating communications, website content, and office technology systems.
* Building collaborative relationships with Member organizations, donors, volunteers, sponsors, and community partners.
* Representing UFAH throughout the community at meetings, fundraising activities, and public events.
* Continuously improving organizational systems, processes, and operational effectiveness.

**Preferred Qualifications**

Successful candidates will typically possess:

* A bachelor's degree or equivalent professional experience in nonprofit administration, fundraising, communications, business administration, arts administration, or a related field.
* Three or more years of progressively responsible experience in nonprofit leadership, administration, fundraising, communications, or organizational operations.
* Excellent written, verbal, organizational, and interpersonal communication skills.
* Experience working collaboratively with nonprofit boards, volunteers, and community partners.
* Strong organizational and project management skills with exceptional attention to detail.
* Experience using donor databases, Microsoft Office, website content management systems, and digital communication platforms.
* The ability to balance strategic leadership with hands\-on operational responsibilities in a small nonprofit environment.

**Work Schedule**

This is a salaried exempt position averaging approximately **24 hours per week** with a flexible work schedule.

Most work occurs during normal weekday business hours. Occasional evening and weekend meetings, fundraising activities, and community events are expected. Limited remote work may be available based on organizational needs and Board expectations.

**Why Join UFAH?**

This is an opportunity to make a meaningful difference in the cultural life of our community.

Working alongside an engaged Board of Directors and dedicated volunteers, the Executive Director will help strengthen a collaborative network of organizations that provide arts education, cultural experiences, historical preservation, and creative opportunities for thousands of people each year.

As UFAH continues implementing its long\-term strategic plan, the Executive Director will play an important role in advancing organizational excellence, strengthening community partnerships, and building a sustainable future for the arts and humanities throughout our region.

How to Apply

Please submit:

* A cover letter describing your interest in the position and relevant qualifications.
* A current résumé.
* The names, relationship, and contact information for three professional references.

**Priority consideration will be given to applications received by August 1, 2026\. The position will remain open until filled.**

References will not be contacted without your knowledge.

Pay: From $37,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule

Work Location: Hybrid remote in La Crosse, WI 54601

jobs.title: Chief Philanthropy Officer (CPO)
jobs.description:
Description:
**Mission \& Vision**


Parc Center for Disabilities is the premier provider of life\-changing services to children and adults with developmental disabilities in the Tampa Bay area. Our vision is a community where all individuals live with dignity and are treated with respect. Our shared purpose is to provide exceptional services to our participants each and every day.

**Position Summary**


The Chief Philanthropy Officer (CPO) is a key member of the Executive Team at Parc Center for Disabilities, serving as a strategic Development and thought leader. This visionary and experienced professional will be responsible for designing and executing innovative fundraising and development strategies that align with the organization's mission and fuel long\-term growth.


With over 15 years of proven success in fundraising, the ideal candidate is a dynamic, results\-driven leader known for surpassing ambitious revenue goals, building transformational donor relationships, and identifying new opportunities for philanthropic engagement. The CPO will bring deep expertise in major gift cultivation, multi\-channel marketing, and strategic partnership development, ensuring the organization's continued financial sustainability and expanded impact.


Reporting directly to the President \& CEO, the CPO will work in close partnership with executive leadership and the Board of Directors. They will inspire and guide a high\-performing Development and Marketing team, elevating Parc Center’s brand, expanding its donor base, and driving mission\-focused initiatives.


Requirements:
**Essential Responsibilities**

* Strategic Leadership: Design and execute comprehensive Development strategies that drive fundraising growth and organizational visibility.
* Major Gifts \& Donor Cultivation: Manage a portfolio of major donors, securing transformational gifts and fostering long\-term donor relationships.
* Donor Engagement: Cultivate authentic relationships with high\-net\-worth individuals and stakeholders, promoting a strong culture of philanthropy.
* Revenue \& Fiscal Growth: Diversify revenue streams through innovative funding opportunities, ensuring financial sustainability.
* Team Leadership \& Development: Mentor and empower a collaborative and high\-performing advancement team.
* Board \& Stakeholder Collaboration: Work alongside Board members and key stakeholders to implement high\-impact fundraising initiatives.
* Research \& Analytics: Use data and insights to inform and refine fundraising strategies, donor engagement and marketing efforts.
* Marketing \& Branding: Oversee and direct contract\-based marketing efforts to enhance visibility and drive public engagement.
* Public Relations \& Communications: Shape strategic messaging that elevates Parc’s brand and thought leadership within the community.
* Other appropriate responsibilities as determined by management.

**Education, Training, Experience, and Other Requirements**

* Minimum of 15 years of progressive leadership experience in nonprofit fundraising and development.
* Proven success in cultivating and securing significant gifts from high\-net\-worth individuals.
* Strong leadership skills with a history of building, mentoring, and managing high\-performing teams.
* Demonstrated experience working closely with Boards, executive teams, and external partners.
* Proficiency in developing and executing multi\-channel marketing and communications strategies.
* Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree or CFRE certification preferred.
* Valid Florida Drivers’ License with acceptable driving record.
* Communicate effectively in English both verbally and in writing.
* Proficient in the Microsoft Office Suite, and other electronic tools
* Parc Center for Disabilities is proud to be a Drug\-Free Workplace.
* Employees must pass Level II background check.

**Schedule**

* Normal business hours are Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Your schedule may vary depending on business needs and other factors.
* This is an on\-site role.

**Pay \& Benefits Package**

* Salary range is $135,000 \- $145,000 annually
* 10 Paid Holidays
* 36 PTO Days and 2 Personal Days
* Health, Dental, \& Vision coverage as well as voluntary supplemental benefits like Short\-Term Disability, Accident, Critical Illness, Life \& AD\&D.
* Health Savings Accounts and Flexible Spending Accounts
* Employee Assistance Program
* Tuition Reimbursement
* 403(b) Retirement Plan

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**Position Summary:** The Executive Director serves as the chief executive and administrative leader of Mariposa House, providing overall strategic, operational, and financial leadership for the organization. This role has ultimate responsibility for organizational oversight, program direction, policy development, fiscal management, personnel leadership, fundraising strategy, and external relations.

The Executive Director works in close partnership with the Board of Directors to advance the agency’s mission, ensure compliance with all legal, regulatory, funding, and contractual requirements, and uphold a trauma\-informed, equitable, and inclusive workplace culture. This position directly supervises staff and is accountable for long\-term sustainability, organizational stability, and the continued development and expansion of high\-quality survivor services across the service region.

**Essential Functions**To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to satisfactorily perform each of the essential responsibilities listed below.

**Leadership \& Organizational Management**

* Provide leadership, oversight, and direction for all programs, services, and daily operations.
* Ensure the organization fulfills all legal, contractual, accreditation, and grant compliance obligations.
* Maintain and implement agency policies and procedures, including confidentiality, mandatory reporting, and ethical standards.
* Lead organizational development and change management efforts to support responsible growth and sustainability.
* Promote a trauma\-informed, equity\-centered approach across all programs and decision\-making.

**Fiscal Management**

* Oversee financial health and long\-term sustainability of the organization.
* Develop and manage annual budgets, financial reporting, compensation planning, and audits in partnership with the Board and fiscal staff.
* Ensure compliance with fiscal policies, funding requirements, and grant management standards.
* Lead efforts to diversify funding streams through grants, partnerships, and fundraising initiatives.

**Program \& Policy Development**

* Develop, implement, and evaluate programs consistent with mission, contractual requirements, and community need.
* Ensure programs remain survivor\-centered, accessible, compliant, and high\-quality.
* Maintain required certifications, professional development, and training standards for self and staff.
* Support advocacy efforts, direct services, prevention initiatives, and community education.

**Board Relations \& Governance**

* Maintain regular, transparent communication with the Board of Directors.
* Provide data, reports, recommendations, and strategic input to support informed board governance and decision\-making.
* Assist the Board in strategic planning and policy development.

**Staff Leadership \& Supervision**

* Provide leadership, coaching, and oversight to supervisory and program staff.
* Foster a collaborative, strengths\-based workplace environment that supports professional development and staff well\-being.
* Support staff in managing vicarious trauma and promote employee retention.
* Uphold high standards of professionalism, accountability, and service delivery.

**Community \& Public Relations**

* Serve as the public representative and spokesperson for the organization.
* Maintain strong relationships with community partners, funders, law enforcement, healthcare providers, Tribes, social service agencies, and other key stakeholders.
* Lead outreach efforts across the service region, including Forks, Hoh River, La Push, Beaver, Clallam Bay, Neah Bay, and surrounding areas.
* Represent the agency in local, regional, and statewide coalitions or workgroups.
* Driving to and from meetings throughout the state.

**Secondary Functions**

Secondary functions of this position include:

* Participate in agency\-wide on\-call responsibilities as scheduled.
* Provide occasional coverage during staff vacancies or leaves of absence.
* Assist with outreach events, fundraising activities, and community engagement efforts.
* Perform other duties as assigned or needed to support organizational success.

**Knowledge \& Skills**

To perform this job successfully, an individual must meet the minimum qualifications listed below. These qualifications are representative of the knowledge, skill and/or ability required to perform this job.

* Strong leadership, collaboration, and communication skills.
* Ability to build and maintain relationships with diverse staff, survivors, community partners, and stakeholders.
* Deep understanding of trauma\-informed practices and the impacts of oppression on survivors.
* Skill in program development, service delivery oversight, and compliance.
* Strong analytical, budgeting, and problem\-solving abilities.
* Ability to balance administrative responsibilities with mission\-driven, field\-based leadership.
* Demonstrated commitment to equity, inclusion, and anti\-oppression values.

**Education \& Experience*****:***

* Master’s degree preferred; equivalent experience will be considered.
* Bachelor’s degree required; equivalent experience will be considered.
* 5–8 years of experience in a senior management or leadership role; nonprofit experience preferred.
* Experience supporting survivors of domestic or sexual violence or working in victim\-services organizations strongly preferred.
* Experience managing grants, contracts, and multi\-funded programs.
* Experience supervising staff and leading diverse teams.

**Certificates and/or Licenses:**

* Valid Washington State Driver’s License and auto insurance required.
* Must meet all training requirements for advocacy and direct service roles as required by Washington State and agency policy.

**Physical Requirements:** 

* Sit or stand for extended periods
* Work at a computer for prolonged periods
* Lift up to 25 lbs. occasionally
* Drive throughout the region for meetings, court support, outreach, and partnership ac

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Lead a Mission. Strengthen a Community. Change Lives.

Springfield Family Center, Inc. is seeking an experienced, compassionate, and visionary Executive Director to lead one of the region's most important charitable organizations.

For more than 50 years, Springfield Family Center has worked alongside community partners to combat food insecurity and provide essential resources to individuals and families throughout the greater Springfield area. Through food distribution, community meals, outreach programs, advocacy, and partnerships, we help ensure that our neighbors have access to nutritious food and supportive services.

We are looking for a dynamic leader who is passionate about community impact and ready to guide our organization into its next chapter of growth and sustainability.

About the Role

Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director serves as the chief executive officer of Springfield Family Center and is responsible for the overall leadership, strategic direction, fundraising, financial management, community relations, and daily operations of the organization.

The Executive Director will work collaboratively with staff, volunteers, donors, community partners, and local leaders to advance our mission and strengthen our impact throughout the region.

Key Responsibilities

Organizational Leadership

* Provide strategic leadership and vision to advance the mission and goals of Springfield Family Center.
* Foster a positive, collaborative, and mission\-driven organizational culture.
* Lead organizational planning and continuous improvement efforts.
* Serve as the primary spokesperson and ambassador for the organization.

Fundraising \& Resource Development

* Develop and implement fundraising strategies to support organizational sustainability.
* Cultivate relationships with donors, foundations, corporations, and community partners.
* Oversee grant writing, grant management, and reporting requirements.
* Identify new funding opportunities and revenue streams.

Financial \& Operational Management

* Manage organizational budgets and financial resources responsibly.
* Ensure compliance with nonprofit regulations, grant requirements, and organizational policies.
* Oversee program operations, including food distribution, community meals, outreach efforts, and volunteer engagement.
* Monitor program outcomes and implement improvements when needed.

Staff Leadership

* Recruit, supervise, mentor, and support staff members.
* Promote professional development and team collaboration.
* Build and maintain a positive workplace culture.

Board \& Community Relations

* Partner closely with the Board of Directors to achieve strategic priorities.
* Provide regular updates regarding organizational performance, finances, and opportunities.
* Strengthen community partnerships and represent Springfield Family Center at public events and meetings.
* Advocate for food security and community well\-being throughout the region.

Qualifications

Required

* Demonstrated success in fundraising, grant development, and donor relations.
* Experience managing budgets and organizational finances.
* Strong supervisory and team leadership experience.
* Excellent communication, relationship\-building, and public speaking skills.

Preferred

* Master's degree in a related field or bachelor’s degree in nonprofit management, business administration, public administration, social services, or a related field.
* Experience in nonprofit leadership.
* Knowledge of food insecurity, hunger relief programs, and community services.
* Experience working with boards of directors and volunteers.

What We Offer

* Opportunity to lead a respected and impactful nonprofit organization.
* Meaningful work that directly improves lives within the community.
* Collaborative Board, staff, and volunteer support.
* Competitive compensation commensurate with experience.
* Professional growth and leadership opportunities.

Submit the following via email

* Cover Letter
* Resume
* Three Professional Reference

Springfield Family Center, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer and encourages applicants from diverse backgrounds and experiences to apply.

Pay: $2,340\.50 \- $2,818\.66 per week

People with a criminal record are encouraged to apply

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Senior Consultant / Executive Director, Client Strategy
jobs.description:
**Position Summary**

The Senior Consultant / Executive Director, Client Strategy will manage a portfolio of nonprofit clients and help them strengthen fundraising systems, develop major gift strategies, conduct feasibility studies, assess campaign readiness, and execute capital or major gift campaigns.

This role is primarily client service and consulting delivery. The right person will be able to lead client meetings, analyze donor and campaign data, advise nonprofit executives, prepare board\-facing materials, and build fundraising strategy for 7 to 8 figure nonprofit campaigns.

The role will also contribute to PRIDE’s internal growth through occasional content development, training materials, webinars, speaking preparation, SOP development, and firm\-wide strategy projects.

This is a full\-time, work from home, remote role with required regular travel.

**About PRIDE Philanthropy**

PRIDE Philanthropy helps nonprofit organizations build clearer, stronger paths to major gifts, campaign commitments, and long\-term fundraising growth. Our clients are often healthcare, hospice, human service, and community\-based nonprofits that need custom fundraising strategy, stronger donor systems, and hands\-on campaign guidance.

We are hiring a consultant who can manage a portfolio of nonprofit clients, lead high\-quality fundraising strategy work, and represent PRIDE with confidence in front of CEOs, development leaders, boards, and campaign volunteers.

This is a client\-facing role for someone who has strategy\-level fundraising judgment, strong communication skills, and the ability to move work forward without needing constant management.

**Core Responsibilities**

**Client Strategy and Consulting**

* Manage a portfolio of approximately 4–6 full\-service nonprofit clients
* Lead client strategy calls, campaign planning meetings, board sessions, and donor pipeline reviews
* Conduct feasibility studies, opportunity assessments, campaign readiness assessments, and major gift strategy engagements
* Help clients identify, prioritize, and move major gift prospects toward meaningful asks
* Develop practical campaign plans, donor engagement strategies, board engagement plans, and fundraising timelines
* Review client fundraising systems, staffing, board structure, donor data, and revenue performance to identify gaps and opportunities
* Prepare clear recommendations and manage clients week\-to\-week

**Campaign and Major Gift Work**

* Support clients through capital campaigns, comprehensive campaigns, major gift initiatives, and special fundraising projects, typically ranging $3M\-$50M
* Help establish campaign goals, gift charts, prospect lists, cultivation plans, solicitation strategies, and volunteer structures
* Coach CEOs, development staff, board members, and campaign volunteers on donor strategy and next steps
* Ensure client work consistently moves toward measurable fundraising outcomes to report to client leadership

**Client Communication and Relationship Management**

* Maintain consistent, clear, and timely communication with clients
* Set expectations, manage timelines, and keep projects moving
* Prepare agendas, meeting notes, recommendations, and follow\-up materials.
* Build trust with client leadership while still being direct about gaps, risks, and underperformance
* Escalate client concerns internally before they become retention problems

**Internal PRIDE Contribution**

* Contribute to PRIDE’s internal systems, templates, training materials, and standard operating procedures
* Support occasional webinars, educational content, speaking preparation, and client\-facing resources
* Participate in team strategy discussions and help improve service delivery across the firm
* Share useful insights from client work that can improve PRIDE’s methodology, sales process, or consulting model

A successful consultant in this role will:

* Retain a strong portfolio of clients through excellent service and clear results
* Keep clients organized, focused, and moving toward fundraising outcomes
* Build trust quickly with CEOs, development leaders, and board members
* Communicate clearly and consistently, both internally and externally
* Manage multiple clients while maintaining quality of service
* Know when to lead, when to listen, and when to push
* Produce work that is polished enough to go in front of a board or executive team
* Strengthen PRIDE’s reputation with every client interaction

**Required Qualifications**

* Significant experience in nonprofit fundraising, advancement, campaign management, or fundraising consulting.
* Demonstrated experience with major gifts, capital campaigns, campaign planning, donor strategy, or development leadership
* Ability to advise nonprofit executives, development staff, and board members
* Strong writing, presentation, facilitation, and client communication skills
* Ability to manage multiple complex projects at once
* Comfort working remotely and independently
* Willingness and ability to travel regularly for client meetings, board sessions, campaign work, and retreats
* Residence within reasonable distance of a commercial airport
* Access to reliable transportation, a professional remote work setup, strong internet, and a high\-quality video meeting environment

**Preferred Qualifications**

* CFRE credential or willingness to earn/maintain CFRE certification
* Experience working with healthcare, hospice, human service, education, or community\-based nonprofits
* Experience serving as a Development Director, Chief Development Officer, Foundation Executive Director, campaign consultant, or senior advancement leader
* Experience facilitating board meetings, campaign committees, retreats, or donor strategy sessions
* Familiarity with donor databases, prospect pipelines, gift charts, campaign reporting, and fundraising dashboards
* Prior consulting experience or demonstrated ability to manage client relationships independently

**Travel Expectations**

*

jobs.title: Ceo
jobs.description:
**Job Overview**

We are seeking an energetic and visionary Chief Executive Officer (CEO) to lead our organization towards sustainable growth and innovation. As the top executive, you will be responsible for setting strategic direction, fostering a high\-performance culture, and ensuring operational excellence across all departments. Your leadership will drive product development, technological advancement, and stakeholder engagement, positioning the company as a leader in its industry. This role offers an exciting opportunity to shape the future of a dynamic organization committed to excellence and innovation.

**Duties**

* Develop and communicate a compelling vision and strategic plan aligned with market trends, leveraging expertise in product strategy, data\-driven decision making, and IT governance.
* Lead cross\-functional teams managing software development projects utilizing Agile methodologies such as SAFe and Waterfall, ensuring timely delivery of high\-quality products.
* Oversee product development lifecycle including requirements gathering, analysis skills, wireframing with tools like Balsamiq, UML modeling, and user acceptance testing to meet stakeholder expectations.
* Manage IT infrastructure, including cloud services like AWS, databases, APIs, and mobile applications to support scalable solutions.
* Drive stakeholder management by engaging with clients, partners, and internal teams to foster B2B sales opportunities and strategic alliances.
* Implement project management practices using tools such as Jira, TFS (Team Foundation Server), VersionOne, Microsoft Office Suite, and project management software for agile planning and tracking.
* Ensure compliance with IT governance standards while promoting innovative system development practices that incorporate SDLC (Software Development Life Cycle) principles.
* Lead strategic initiatives in emerging technologies like Internet of Things (IoT), SaaS platforms, Looker analytics tools, and system development product management to enhance business capabilities.

**Qualifications**

* Proven experience in executive leadership roles with a strong background in software development, product management, or IT infrastructure management.
* Extensive knowledge of Agile software development frameworks such as SAFe and Agile planning methodologies.
* Demonstrated expertise in project management methodologies including Waterfall and Scrum; familiarity with TFS, Jira, VersionOne is preferred.
* Technical proficiency in programming languages like Java; experience with HTML, SQL, APIs; understanding of UML modeling; proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint and Excel for strategic presentations and data analysis.
* Strong analysis skills for requirements design and requirements gathering; ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear business strategies.
* Excellent stakeholder management skills with a focus on leadership, communication skills, and strategic management capabilities.
* Familiarity with IT governance standards to ensure compliance across all technology initiatives.
* Ability to lead technical product cross\-functional teams effectively while managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment.

Join us if you’re ready to lead with passion! Bring your expertise in product development projects management, data analytics, cloud solutions like AWS, SaaS platforms, and innovative system development practices to make a lasting impact on our organization’s future growth!

Pay: $62,429\.43 \- $75,183\.84 per year

Work Location: Remote

jobs.title: Director of Advancement & Engagement
jobs.description:
Part Time Contractor

Position Summary

Friends of Jones (FOJ) seeks a strategic, entrepreneurial, and relationship\-focused Director of Advancement and Engagement to support the long\-term growth and sustainability of Jones College Prep. Reporting to the Fundraising Chair of Friends of Jones and working in close partnership with Jones College Prep leadership, this position will lead advancement initiatives that strengthen relationships with families, alumni, donors, and corporate partners. This is a contract position.

The Director of Advancement will establish and manage constituent engagement, donor stewardship, fundraising support, and corporate partnerships. This role will also provide project management support for initiatives jointly sponsored by the Friends of Jones Fundraising team.

Key Responsibilities

Student, Family, and Alumni Data Management

* Establish processes with Chicago Public Schools and school administration to obtain and maintain contact information for newly accepted and enrolled Jones students and families, consistent with applicable privacy requirements.
* Develop and maintain systems to track student enrollment trends, including students' originating middle schools.
* Create and manage an alumni database, including collection and maintenance of information related to college attendance and majors.

Fundraising and Donor Management

* Collaborate with the Friends of Jones fundraising team to strengthen donor engagement and increase philanthropic support.
* In tandem with Fundraising chair develop the annual fundraising plan. This includes but is not limited to:
* Developing goals for the Flying Eagles Annual Fund
* Develop tactical plan for fundraising – communication plan, events, outreach targets, segmentation approach
* Lead the execution of the tactical plans in conjunction with the fundraising chair and the fundraising team
* Work with the comms team to deliver fundraising messages to the Jones community
* Maintain donor records and oversee donor tracking, stewardship, and recognition programs.
* Work with treasurer to ensure fundraising reports and dashboards are accurate – in line with expectations

Corporate and Community Partnerships

* Develop and launch a corporate outreach and partnership program to connect Jones College Prep with local and national businesses.
* Identify, cultivate, and steward corporate sponsors and strategic partners.
* Create sponsorship opportunities that support academic programs, student experiences, events, and other school priorities.
* Identify other community groups for possible fundraising (i.e. chambers of commerce)
* Work with Friends of Jones leadership to establish goals, strategies, and processes for corporate engagement.

Strategic Projects and School Advancement

* Support strategic communications and marketing initiatives, including potential enhancements to the Jones website and digital presence.
* Coordinate stakeholders, vendors, volunteers, to ensure successful project fundraising execution.

Collaboration and Leadership

* Work closely with Friends of Jones leadership, school administration, volunteers, parents, alumni, and community partners.
* Develop annual advancement goals and measurable outcomes.
* Provide regular updates on progress, challenges, and opportunities.
* Foster a collaborative culture between Friends of Jones and Jones College Prep in support of shared advancement objectives.
* Attend monthly Friends of Jones Board meetings
* Attend weekly fundraising meetings

Alumni Relations and Engagement

* In tandem with alumni coordinator, support ongoing alumni outreach efforts and collaborate with designated alumni leaders.
* Assist in building a culture of lifelong engagement and philanthropy among Jones graduates.

Qualifications

* Bachelor's degree preferred.
* Experience in fundraising, advancement, nonprofit management, alumni relations, communications, education, or a related field.
* Strong project management and organizational skills.
* Experience managing constituent or donor databases and reporting systems.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Demonstrated ability to build relationships with diverse stakeholders.
* Ability to work independently while managing multiple priorities.
* Experience with CRM, fundraising, or alumni engagement platforms preferred.

Schedule and Compensation

* Part\-time contract position (approximately 20–25 hours per week; flexible based on organizational needs).
* Some evening and weekend participation will be required for events and meetings.
* Compensation commensurate with experience.

Reporting Structure

The Director of Advancement reports jointly to the President and Fundraising Chair of Friends of Jones and works closely with Jones College Prep leadership to advance shared fundraising, alumni engagement, and strategic development goals.

Pay: $40,000\.00 \- $50,000\.00 per year

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Chicago, IL 60605

jobs.title: Executive Director of Advancing Whole Health
jobs.description:
Are you a visionary leader focused on Community Wellness? Is your passion to create a place for all people to thrive through programs, partnerships and volunteer experiences that foster achievement, relationships and belonging? Apply to be the **Executive Director of Advancing Whole Health**!


Responsibilities

* Represent the Y in the community by developing and maintaining positive relationships with members, staff, local organizations, and community members.
* Expand and innovate initiatives and programs for Advancing Whole Health through partnerships and resource development.
* Lead and develop program logistics, records, and program evaluation reports while managing budgets, forecasting, and department sustainability.
* Lead strategic plan goals and marketing efforts to promote Y programs and services across the teams of Impact and Advocacy, Experience and Advancement.
* Cultivate and sustain partnerships with local businesses, organizations, and stakeholders to enhance community impact and program initiatives.


Knowledge \& Skills  

Should have strong leadership, strategic planning, and financial management skills, along with expertise in organizational development and stakeholder engagement. Must possess knowledge of industry trends, advocacy, and team management to drive mission\-aligned growth and sustainability. A high school diploma or equivalent is required, with a bachelor's degree in a related field preferred. Candidates must have at least five years of community outreach experience and proficiency in Windows\-based software such as Daxko, Outlook, Word, and Excel. Strong organizational, relationship\-building, and communication skills are essential, along with supervisory, administrative, and financial management abilities. Some evening and weekend work is required to oversee programs.


Why Join Us?  

The YMCA of Greater Richmond stands committed to creating a work environment comprised of individuals from diverse socio\-economic, racial, ethnic, religious, and national origins. We believe in nurturing the potential of all staff and are dedicated to the ongoing professional development of talent. We believe a diverse and inclusive work community is essential to fulfilling our mission of being open to all. The Y is a cause\-driven organization focused on strengthening our community. The principles that guide us in our work are our core values of caring, honesty, respect, and responsibility. Be part of an organization that is best in class for member experience, team development, and community impact. Apply today and make a difference!


Compensation and Benefits  

**Salary is $87,500 per year or based on experience and qualifications.** We offer an exciting, fun, inclusive, growth\-oriented work environment that includes strong health benefits options, 12% employer\-paid retirement upon meeting eligibility, childcare discount, complimentary Y membership, and continuous professional development.

jobs.title: Cultural Affairs Director
jobs.description:
**The position is located in Covered Wells, AZ or San Xavier AZ.**

**Position Summary:**


Under general direction, responsible for the compilation of pre\-existing Tohono O'odham recorded media as well as originating programming which promotes knowledge of Tohono O'odham Culture and use of the Tohono O'odham Language and is responsible or delegates the responsibility to translate regularly scheduled news programs.


The work is normally reviewed upon completion to ensure compliance with general organization/department goals, policies and procedures. Major work activities are covered by procedures or guidelines permitting flexibility in selecting methods to utilize and procedures to follow, determination of work priorities, and the planning and coordination of projects.


**Essential Duties and Responsibilities:**

* Reviews and recommends programming policies, procedures, systems and forms designed to improve cultural programming.
* Provides leadership and training to student D.Js; assist students with announcements, editing material, review and prepare material to air.
* Responsible for the translation of the daily news reports into Tohono O'odham Language.
* Originates programming emphasizing Tohono O'odham Himdag and Culture.
* Assists in preparing annual budget and budget modifications regarding KOHN programming.
* Ensures compliance with the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) regulations and the standards set by management.
* Maintains individual and departmental calendars, appointments, events; coordinates and arranges meetings as required.
* Establishes and approves materials broadcast on radio stations.
* Evaluates materials prior to broadcast for accuracy, quality and adherence to federal guidelines; maintains quality control of broadcast material.
* Monitors broadcast traffic and logging activities and makes improvements when necessary to enhance programming format.
* Schedules local promotional material and public service announcements.
* Previews and screens local and regional program sources for possible station use and participates in purchase of programs.
* Participates in the development of station goals, policies and procedures.
* Analyzes the programming needs of the listening public and creates programs to serve needs; assigns staff producers to create programs.
* Serves as producer/host of various music and news or other information programming as assigned.
* Represents the station in responding to audience programming inquiries. Attends all regular management meetings.
* Represents KOHN in a positive and professional manner.
* Establishes, posts, and keeps reasonable office hours.
* Creates correspondence, memos, minutes, agendas or reports from drafts, recordings, or verbal instruction.
* Maintains professional and technical knowledge by conducting research, attending seminars, educational workshops, classes and conferences; reviewing professional publications; establishing networks; participating in professional societies; conferring with representatives of contracting agencies and related organizations.
* Reports directly to the General Manager or designee.
* Performs other job related duties as assigned and contributes to a team effort.

**Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:**

* Knowledge of the Tohono O'odham culture, customs, and traditions.
* Knowledge of applicable federal, state, tribal laws, regulations and requirements.
* Knowledge of journalistic writing, spelling, grammar and punctuation.
* Knowledge of production techniques, procedures and standards.
* Knowledge of broadcast rules, regulations and guidelines.
* Knowledge of programming techniques, procedures and standards.
* Knowledge of the program format subject area.
* Skill in providing superior customer service to internal and external customers.
* Skill in effective leadership and supervision.
* Skill in communication, presentation and program planning.
* Ability to work extended hours and various work schedules including holidays.
* Ability to learn how to operate studio equipment, radio automation systems, and database software programs.
* Ability to communicate efficiently and effectively both verbally and in writing.
* Ability to handle multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
* Ability to exercise independent judgment in the exercise of job responsibilities.
* Ability to plan, assign, and review the work of assigned personnel and volunteers.
* Ability to define problems, collect data, establish facts and draw valid conclusions.
* Ability to effectively translate to and from Tohono O'odham to English.
* Ability to establish and maintain positive and effective working relationships with other employees, and the general public.
* Ability to maintain privileged confidential information.
* Ability to travel within the interior/exterior boundaries of the Tohono O'odham Nation.

**Minimum Qualifications:**

* Bachelor's Degree in Broadcasting or closely related field, and one year work experience in program evaluation, acquisition and scheduling for radio or television, or an equivalent combination of training, education, and work experience which demonstrates the ability to perform the duties of this position.
* One year of supervisory experience.

**Licenses, Certifications, Special Requirements:**

* Demonstrated knowledge of Tohono O'odham Himdag.
* Upon recommendation for hire, a criminal background check is required to determine suitability for hire, including a 39\-month Motor Vehicle Record.
* May require possessing and maintaining a valid driver's license, (no DUIs or major traffic citations within the last three years).
* If required, must meet the Tohono O'odham Nation tribal employer's insurance requirements to receive a driver's permit to operate program vehicles.
* Based on the department's needs, incumbents may be required to demonstrate fluency in both the Tohono O'odham Language and English as a condition of employment.

new_jobs.title: Deputy Director of Recreation & Community Services
new_jobs.description:
**Description**
---------------


**POSITION:**
The Deputy Director of Recreation \& Community Services is a key leadership role responsible for overseeing the Department’s daily operations and supporting the Director of Recreation \& Community Services in strategic planning and program delivery. This position provides high\-level managerial and administrative support in the development, implementation, and evaluation of City programs, including recreation services, park programming and development, Capital Improvement Projects (CIP), community and social services, events, and facility rentals.  

The Deputy Director manages the day\-to\-day operations and plans, directs, and evaluates departmental programs, services, facilities, and operations; supervises professional and administrative staff; and ensures effective coordination across City departments and external partners. The position also serves as acting Director when designated and acts as the liaison to assigned commissions and committees.  

This role requires flexibility, including evening and weekend work, and occasional travel.
**Examples of Duties**
----------------------


**ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:**

* Under the general direction of the Director of Recreation \& Community Services, oversee the delivery of recreation programs, community services, events, and facility operations.
* Oversee front desk operations, including registration, deposits, balancing, and customer service.
* Administer the Department event calendar and manage the Community Calendar.
* Assist in managing facility maintenance and department vehicles.
* Assist in the development and implementation of departmental goals, policies, procedures, and performance standards.
* Monitor and evaluate program effectiveness, service quality, and customer experience; implement improvements and risk management practices.
* Coordinate major departmental initiatives, projects, and capital improvements; stay current with industry trends and best practices.
* Supervise, mentor, and evaluate staff; recommend hiring, discipline, and performance standards; and oversee training and professional development programs.
* Participate in and oversee the preparation and administration of departmental budgets, including revenue projections, cost recovery analysis, grants, and expenditure control.
* Provide oversight of park and facility operations and ensure alignment with City standards and community needs.
* Review and approve final departmental marketing and communications.
* Serve as a liaison to assigned advisory boards, community groups, and other stakeholders; provide technical guidance and support.
* Research, prepare, and present reports, recommendations, and presentations to the City Council, commissions, and committees.
* Analyze complex operational and community issues; develop and implement effective solutions.
* Conduct and lead the team in an annual Department program and event evaluation.
* Exercises sound judgment, prioritizes tasks, and maintains political awareness.
* Analyzes complex issues related to parks and recreation.
* Report, research, and participate in advocacy in parks and recreation legislation.
* Foster partnerships and coordinate activities with other City departments, agencies, and community organizations.
* Assists the Director in community representation at external activities, functions, and events. Attend internal City events in a department lead capacity.
* Act on behalf of the Director when designated.
* This role requires flexibility, including evening and weekend work, and occasional travel.
* Engages with the public and collaborates with Citizen advisory boards, Community Foundation, vendors and partners, City and community officials.
* Performs additional duties as assigned.

**Typical Qualifications**
--------------------------


**EXPERIENCE AND TRAINING:**
Graduation from an accredited college or university with major course work in recreation, public administration or related field with eight (8\) years of increasingly responsible full\-time experience, including four (4\) years at full\-time Manager level experience in the development and management of municipal recreation and community and social service programs, event management, and parks and recreation facilities. A Master's degree in Public Administration or Recreation Administration is highly desirable. **DESIRABLE KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES****Knowledge of:*** Principles, practices, and methods used in parks and recreation management.
* Organizational and management practices as applied to the analysis and evaluation of programs, policies, and operational needs.
* Pertinent federal, state and local laws, rules, codes, and ordinances.
* Methods and techniques of effective project management, and technical report preparation and presentation.
* Principles and practices of contract negotiation, preparation, and administration.
* Principles and practices of program organization, evaluation, development, and administration.
* Recent developments, contemporary trends and practices of parks, recreation, arts, and events.
* Operational characteristics, services and activities of recreational and community service programs.
* Principles and practices of budget preparation and administration.
* Principles and practices of personnel management, supervision, training, and performance evaluation.
* Principles and practices of leadership, motivation, team building, and conflict resolution.
* Modern office practices and technology, including personal computer hardware and software; computer\-based project management and scheduling.
* Safe work practices.
* English usage, spelling, grammar, and punctuation.

**Ability to****:*** Plan, direct, manage, and oversee the administration and operations of the Recreation \& Community Services Department, venue rentals, arts, events, programs, and activities.
* Develop, implement, and interpret goals, objectives, policies, procedures, and work stan

new_jobs.title: After-School Program Director (MUST HAVE PRIOR DYCD COMPASS SUPERVISORY EXPERIENCE)
new_jobs.description:
Organization Overview

Veronica Arts Media, Inc. is a 501c3 nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering youth through high\-quality enrichment programs that support academic success, creativity, wellness, and personal growth. Our mission is to provide students with access to engaging programs such as physical fitness, academic support, social\-emotional learning, STEAM and literacy enrichment, media arts, leadership development, and early exposure to college and career pathways. To learn more about Veronica Arts Media, visit www.veronicaartsmedia.org.

Why Join Veronica Arts Media, Inc.?

This is your chance to contribute directly to an organization committed to empowering youth through high\-quality enrichment, creativity, wellness, academic support, and life\-changing opportunities.

Position Overview

The Program Director oversees the daily operations of a COMPASS after\-school program and provides leadership and supervision to staff to ensure program goals and student outcomes are achieved. As the on\-site leader, the Program Director is responsible for the high\-quality implementation of a DYCD\-funded program (COMPASS/SONYC), with a focus on participant safety, regulatory compliance (DYCD, SACC, and organizational standards), and fiscal accountability. The Program Director serves as the primary liaison with the school principal and represents the program within the broader school community, including NYCPS staff, families, and community partners. The role leads the delivery of a dynamic program that includes academic enrichment, project\-based learning, arts, sports, leadership development, and initiatives supporting students’ academic and social\-emotional growth, including NYC Reads. This role also includes leading our Summer Rising programming each year, requiring flexibility with scheduling and location adjustments during the summer months. This is a full\-time position based in the Bronx. The school\-year schedule is Monday–Friday, 10:00 AM–6:00 PM, with additional evening and weekend availability required based on program needs.The Program Director reports to the Executive Director and Site Supervisor.

Key Responsibilities

* Ensure program activities align with DYCD COMPASS and SONYC requirements and organizational goals.
* Oversee daily site operations to maintain a safe, structured, and compliant program environment.
* Manage attendance tracking, data entry, and required documentation in DYCD Connect.
* Supervise and support site staff, including the Content Specialist, Group Leaders, and Activity Specialists.
* Conduct staff observations and evaluations and provide ongoing coaching and professional development.
* Ensure compliance with SACC regulations, including supervision ratios, safety protocols, and staff credential requirements.
* Serve as the primary point of contact with NYCPS school personnel, families, and community partners.
* Lead recruitment and retention efforts to meet DYCD enrollment and attendance targets.
* Support high‑quality programming across literacy, STEM, arts, civic engagement, and NYC Reads initiatives.
* Coordinate inclusion supports and manage Individualized Accommodation Plans (IAPs) for participants with diverse needs.
* Lead the implementation of safety drills, behavior protocols, and crisis management plans.

Requirements

* DOE Fingerprinted
* SACC Director Requirement
* Statewide Central Registry clearance
* Department of Health clearance
* Bachelor’s degree in a child\-related field (e.g., Education, Child Development, Psychology, Social Work) and at least two years of direct experience working with school\-age children; or A minimum of three (3\) years of supervisory or program management experience in youth development or educational settings.
* Two years of college with 18 credits in Child Development, Elementary Education, Physical Education, Recreation or a related field

Qualifications

* Knowledge of DYCD COMPASS and SONYC program requirements and experience working in partnership with NYCPS schools.
* Demonstrated ability to supervise staff, manage program operations, oversee budgets, and maintain regulatory compliance.
* Experience collaborating with school staff, families, and community partners.
* Strong communication, organizational, and problem\-solving skills, with a commitment to youth development, student safety, educational equity, and culturally responsive practices.

Approach to Work

* Strong commitment to educational equity
* Passion for working with students in under\-resourced schools
* Exceptional level of patience and focus to overcome challenges and achieve results

Pay and Benefits

* Commensurate with experience
* Sick and Safe Leave
* Health, dental, and vision insurance
* Select DOE holidays, depending on OST program operating schedules

Pay: From $65,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

new_jobs.company: United Fund for the Arts and Humanities
new_jobs.title: Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
**Help Shape the Future of Arts and Humanities in Our Region**

The United Fund for the Arts and Humanities (UFAH) brings people together to strengthen the arts and humanities through collaborative fundraising.

Each year, UFAH works with donors, businesses, foundations, volunteers, and partner organizations to support programs that inspire creativity, preserve history, expand arts education, and enrich community life throughout the greater La Crosse area.

As Executive Director, you will help lead this work by strengthening community relationships, coordinating fundraising efforts, supporting good governance, and ensuring the organization operates with professionalism, accountability, and excellence.

**Position Summary**

The Executive Director serves as UFAH's primary staff leader and works closely with the Board of Directors to advance the organization's mission and strategic priorities.

This is a hands\-on leadership position within a one\-staff organization. The Executive Director provides organizational leadership while coordinating fundraising activities, supporting Board governance, managing day\-to\-day operations, maintaining administrative systems, and building productive relationships throughout the community.

Supported by an engaged Board of Directors, active committees, and dedicated volunteers, the Executive Director plays a central role in ensuring UFAH remains organized, responsive, and sustainable.

**Primary Responsibilities**

The Executive Director is responsible for:

* Coordinating UFAH's Annual Fund and donor stewardship activities.
* Managing day\-to\-day organizational operations and office administration.
* Supporting the Board of Directors and its committees through meeting coordination, governance support, and implementation of Board\-approved policies.
* Maintaining accurate donor, financial, and organizational records.
* Coordinating communications, website content, and office technology systems.
* Building collaborative relationships with Member organizations, donors, volunteers, sponsors, and community partners.
* Representing UFAH throughout the community at meetings, fundraising activities, and public events.
* Continuously improving organizational systems, processes, and operational effectiveness.

**Preferred Qualifications**

Successful candidates will typically possess:

* A bachelor's degree or equivalent professional experience in nonprofit administration, fundraising, communications, business administration, arts administration, or a related field.
* Three or more years of progressively responsible experience in nonprofit leadership, administration, fundraising, communications, or organizational operations.
* Excellent written, verbal, organizational, and interpersonal communication skills.
* Experience working collaboratively with nonprofit boards, volunteers, and community partners.
* Strong organizational and project management skills with exceptional attention to detail.
* Experience using donor databases, Microsoft Office, website content management systems, and digital communication platforms.
* The ability to balance strategic leadership with hands\-on operational responsibilities in a small nonprofit environment.

**Work Schedule**

This is a salaried exempt position averaging approximately **24 hours per week** with a flexible work schedule.

Most work occurs during normal weekday business hours. Occasional evening and weekend meetings, fundraising activities, and community events are expected. Limited remote work may be available based on organizational needs and Board expectations.

**Why Join UFAH?**

This is an opportunity to make a meaningful difference in the cultural life of our community.

Working alongside an engaged Board of Directors and dedicated volunteers, the Executive Director will help strengthen a collaborative network of organizations that provide arts education, cultural experiences, historical preservation, and creative opportunities for thousands of people each year.

As UFAH continues implementing its long\-term strategic plan, the Executive Director will play an important role in advancing organizational excellence, strengthening community partnerships, and building a sustainable future for the arts and humanities throughout our region.

How to Apply

Please submit:

* A cover letter describing your interest in the position and relevant qualifications.
* A current résumé.
* The names, relationship, and contact information for three professional references.

**Priority consideration will be given to applications received by August 1, 2026\. The position will remain open until filled.**

References will not be contacted without your knowledge.

Pay: From $37,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule

Work Location: Hybrid remote in La Crosse, WI 54601

new_jobs.title: Chief Philanthropy Officer (CPO)
new_jobs.description:
Description:
**Mission \& Vision**


Parc Center for Disabilities is the premier provider of life\-changing services to children and adults with developmental disabilities in the Tampa Bay area. Our vision is a community where all individuals live with dignity and are treated with respect. Our shared purpose is to provide exceptional services to our participants each and every day.

**Position Summary**


The Chief Philanthropy Officer (CPO) is a key member of the Executive Team at Parc Center for Disabilities, serving as a strategic Development and thought leader. This visionary and experienced professional will be responsible for designing and executing innovative fundraising and development strategies that align with the organization's mission and fuel long\-term growth.


With over 15 years of proven success in fundraising, the ideal candidate is a dynamic, results\-driven leader known for surpassing ambitious revenue goals, building transformational donor relationships, and identifying new opportunities for philanthropic engagement. The CPO will bring deep expertise in major gift cultivation, multi\-channel marketing, and strategic partnership development, ensuring the organization's continued financial sustainability and expanded impact.


Reporting directly to the President \& CEO, the CPO will work in close partnership with executive leadership and the Board of Directors. They will inspire and guide a high\-performing Development and Marketing team, elevating Parc Center’s brand, expanding its donor base, and driving mission\-focused initiatives.


Requirements:
**Essential Responsibilities**

* Strategic Leadership: Design and execute comprehensive Development strategies that drive fundraising growth and organizational visibility.
* Major Gifts \& Donor Cultivation: Manage a portfolio of major donors, securing transformational gifts and fostering long\-term donor relationships.
* Donor Engagement: Cultivate authentic relationships with high\-net\-worth individuals and stakeholders, promoting a strong culture of philanthropy.
* Revenue \& Fiscal Growth: Diversify revenue streams through innovative funding opportunities, ensuring financial sustainability.
* Team Leadership \& Development: Mentor and empower a collaborative and high\-performing advancement team.
* Board \& Stakeholder Collaboration: Work alongside Board members and key stakeholders to implement high\-impact fundraising initiatives.
* Research \& Analytics: Use data and insights to inform and refine fundraising strategies, donor engagement and marketing efforts.
* Marketing \& Branding: Oversee and direct contract\-based marketing efforts to enhance visibility and drive public engagement.
* Public Relations \& Communications: Shape strategic messaging that elevates Parc’s brand and thought leadership within the community.
* Other appropriate responsibilities as determined by management.

**Education, Training, Experience, and Other Requirements**

* Minimum of 15 years of progressive leadership experience in nonprofit fundraising and development.
* Proven success in cultivating and securing significant gifts from high\-net\-worth individuals.
* Strong leadership skills with a history of building, mentoring, and managing high\-performing teams.
* Demonstrated experience working closely with Boards, executive teams, and external partners.
* Proficiency in developing and executing multi\-channel marketing and communications strategies.
* Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree or CFRE certification preferred.
* Valid Florida Drivers’ License with acceptable driving record.
* Communicate effectively in English both verbally and in writing.
* Proficient in the Microsoft Office Suite, and other electronic tools
* Parc Center for Disabilities is proud to be a Drug\-Free Workplace.
* Employees must pass Level II background check.

**Schedule**

* Normal business hours are Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Your schedule may vary depending on business needs and other factors.
* This is an on\-site role.

**Pay \& Benefits Package**

* Salary range is $135,000 \- $145,000 annually
* 10 Paid Holidays
* 36 PTO Days and 2 Personal Days
* Health, Dental, \& Vision coverage as well as voluntary supplemental benefits like Short\-Term Disability, Accident, Critical Illness, Life \& AD\&D.
* Health Savings Accounts and Flexible Spending Accounts
* Employee Assistance Program
* Tuition Reimbursement
* 403(b) Retirement Plan

new_jobs.title: Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
**Position Summary:** The Executive Director serves as the chief executive and administrative leader of Mariposa House, providing overall strategic, operational, and financial leadership for the organization. This role has ultimate responsibility for organizational oversight, program direction, policy development, fiscal management, personnel leadership, fundraising strategy, and external relations.

The Executive Director works in close partnership with the Board of Directors to advance the agency’s mission, ensure compliance with all legal, regulatory, funding, and contractual requirements, and uphold a trauma\-informed, equitable, and inclusive workplace culture. This position directly supervises staff and is accountable for long\-term sustainability, organizational stability, and the continued development and expansion of high\-quality survivor services across the service region.

**Essential Functions**To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to satisfactorily perform each of the essential responsibilities listed below.

**Leadership \& Organizational Management**

* Provide leadership, oversight, and direction for all programs, services, and daily operations.
* Ensure the organization fulfills all legal, contractual, accreditation, and grant compliance obligations.
* Maintain and implement agency policies and procedures, including confidentiality, mandatory reporting, and ethical standards.
* Lead organizational development and change management efforts to support responsible growth and sustainability.
* Promote a trauma\-informed, equity\-centered approach across all programs and decision\-making.

**Fiscal Management**

* Oversee financial health and long\-term sustainability of the organization.
* Develop and manage annual budgets, financial reporting, compensation planning, and audits in partnership with the Board and fiscal staff.
* Ensure compliance with fiscal policies, funding requirements, and grant management standards.
* Lead efforts to diversify funding streams through grants, partnerships, and fundraising initiatives.

**Program \& Policy Development**

* Develop, implement, and evaluate programs consistent with mission, contractual requirements, and community need.
* Ensure programs remain survivor\-centered, accessible, compliant, and high\-quality.
* Maintain required certifications, professional development, and training standards for self and staff.
* Support advocacy efforts, direct services, prevention initiatives, and community education.

**Board Relations \& Governance**

* Maintain regular, transparent communication with the Board of Directors.
* Provide data, reports, recommendations, and strategic input to support informed board governance and decision\-making.
* Assist the Board in strategic planning and policy development.

**Staff Leadership \& Supervision**

* Provide leadership, coaching, and oversight to supervisory and program staff.
* Foster a collaborative, strengths\-based workplace environment that supports professional development and staff well\-being.
* Support staff in managing vicarious trauma and promote employee retention.
* Uphold high standards of professionalism, accountability, and service delivery.

**Community \& Public Relations**

* Serve as the public representative and spokesperson for the organization.
* Maintain strong relationships with community partners, funders, law enforcement, healthcare providers, Tribes, social service agencies, and other key stakeholders.
* Lead outreach efforts across the service region, including Forks, Hoh River, La Push, Beaver, Clallam Bay, Neah Bay, and surrounding areas.
* Represent the agency in local, regional, and statewide coalitions or workgroups.
* Driving to and from meetings throughout the state.

**Secondary Functions**

Secondary functions of this position include:

* Participate in agency\-wide on\-call responsibilities as scheduled.
* Provide occasional coverage during staff vacancies or leaves of absence.
* Assist with outreach events, fundraising activities, and community engagement efforts.
* Perform other duties as assigned or needed to support organizational success.

**Knowledge \& Skills**

To perform this job successfully, an individual must meet the minimum qualifications listed below. These qualifications are representative of the knowledge, skill and/or ability required to perform this job.

* Strong leadership, collaboration, and communication skills.
